author: WSvX
content:
## Workload assessment: DiffUtil could regress performance

Full DiffUtil migration is likely a net negative under realistic workloads:

- notifyDataSetChanged() only rebinds visible ViewHolders (~5-10 items), not the entire list
- DiffUtil.calculateDiff() scans the ENTIRE list — O(N) best case, O(N^2) worst case
- The hashCode() guard at EventListAdapter.kt:115 already skips identical updates
- Multi-type adapter (ListEvent/ListSectionDay/ListSectionMonth) frequently hits worst-case diff path on view changes

Realistic estimates (DiffUtil vs current):
- Day view (10-20 items): ~1ms saved — negligible
- Week view (30-50 items): break-even
- Month/list (100-200 items): 5-15ms SLOWER
- Widget 1yr (500+ items): 50-200ms SLOWER (O(N^2) path)

Additional risks:
- Parent class MyRecyclerViewAdapter provides CAB selection that doesn't integrate with ListAdapter.submitList() async diffing
- ListEvent.hashCode() (14 fields) as selection key could reference stale positions during async diff
- Would require forking org.fossify.commons or wrapping the adapter

Revised recommendation: 2-line fix only — replace notifyDataSetChanged() with notifyItemRangeChanged() in toggle24HourFormat() and togglePrintMode(). No DiffUtil, no inheritance change. See .agent-workspace/upstream-issues/0017-replace-notifydatasetchanged-with-diffutil.md for full analysis.
